zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Cornhall於大約1140年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Gervase de Cornhill18歲，母親Agnes Cornhill27歲。她於大約1205年與william de Wrotham在英國英格蘭諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Cornhall于大约1140年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲Gervase de Cornhill18岁，母亲Agnes Cornhill27岁。她于大约1205年与william de Wrotham在英国英格兰諾福爾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Cornhall was born about 1140, in Devon, England, her father, Gervase de Cornhill, was 18 and her mother, Agnes Cornhill, was 27. She married william de Wrotham about 1205, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Cornhall est née environ 1140, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Gervase de Cornhill, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Agnes Cornhill, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé william de Wrotham environ 1205, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Cornhall um 1140 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gervase de Cornhill 18 und ihre Mutter Agnes Cornhill 27. Sie hat um 1205 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom william de Wrotham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Cornhall è nata il circa 1140 nel Devon, England, sua padre, Gervase de Cornhill, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Agnes Cornhill, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato william de Wrotham circa 1205, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Cornhall(이)가 대략 1140년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gervase de Cornhill(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Cornhill(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1205년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 william de Wrotham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Cornhall nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gervase de Cornhill, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Cornhill, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com william de Wrotham aproximadamente 1205, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Cornhall родилась около 1140 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Gervase de Cornhill было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Cornhill, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени william de Wrotham около 1205 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Cornhall nació aproximadamente 1140, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Gervase de Cornhill, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Agnes Cornhill, tenía 27 años. Se casó con william de Wrotham aproximadamente 1205, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
